title: Passerina truncata
taxonomy: Passerina truncata is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, commonly known as the Jacob's ladder or Greek valerian. It is native to the southwestern United States. This plant is classified as a perennial, meaning it lives for more than two years. Passerina truncata is further classified as a member of the genus Passerina, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas. The specific epithet "truncata" refers to the plant's truncated or cut-off leaves.

preparations: Passerina truncata, also known as Blue Cardinal Flower, is a plant that has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties.
